hypothesis: F-EVAL1 closure is PREMATURE: composite score unstable (Increase at 3.3% margin), only 2 post-fix measurements, glass ceiling masks zero EXCELLENT goals with genuine external grounding.
method: Adversarial audit: (1) count independent measurement points, (2) map score trajectory across all 16 measurements, (3) analyze Increase stability margin, (4) assess post-fix regime validity, (5) test whether original question is definitively answerable independent of limitations.
expect: >=2 of 4 closure dimensions fail: stability, external grounding, post-fix confirmation.
actual: 3/4 adversarial criteria HIT: Increase fragile (3.3% margin, DOMEX-dependent), external grounding blocked (5% frozen 138 sessions), post-fix only 2 measurements. BUT the original question ('can we compute?') IS definitively answered YES with 16 data points across 285 sessions. Computation mechanism works; limitations are quality issues, not existence issues. B-EVAL1 hypothesis CONFIRMED: 12/12 metrics self-referential (L-1211). Tool functional since S193.
diff: Expected closure premature: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED (3/4 concerns valid). But question-level resolution IS possible because the question is 'can we compute' not 'is the score perfect'. Adversarial contribution: documented that Increase is DOMEX-burst-dependent (avg_lp=2.79 DOMEX vs 1.74 non-DOMEX; non-DOMEX alone = INADEQUATE). Novel: score rebalancing attractor means goal-level instability coexists with composite-level stability.
resolution: CONFIRMED with limitations. Tool works, composite sustained SUFFICIENT 2.0-2.25/3 over 16 measurements (S403-S478). Limitations: (1) glass ceiling 2.0-2.25/3 without F-COMP1, (2) Increase DOMEX-dependent, (3) all metrics self-referential. Successor questions: F-EVAL2 (external grounding gap), F-EVAL3 (minimum improvement rate), F-COMP1 (external validation infrastructure), F-GND1 (trail provenance).
